summary: Surah ‘Abasa gently corrects the Prophet for frowning when a blind seeker came, teaching that sincere seekers deserve attention over the heedless. The reminder (Qur’an) is on exalted, purified pages written by noble scribes. Man is rebuked for ingratitude despite being created from a drop, measured, eased, then caused to die and be buried, and raised when Allah wills. Signs in provision—rain, split earth, grains, fruits, gardens—are cited. The Deafening Blast will make people flee from closest kin; faces will be bright and rejoicing, while others will be dust-covered and dark—those are the disbelievers, sinners.
